title: MP Eva Souls
wikitext:
[]: MP Eva Souls Post by VoidEater » Mon Jan 26, 2009 1:36 pm Are souls required for the MP Evas? It could be a case of a soul is always necessary to activate an Eva, even an MP Eva. Or could it be a case of the body responding to Kaworu's Dummy Plug - since Kaworu can only sync with an Eva whose resident soul is in hiding ("not present"), might a resident soul in the case of the MP Evas be unnecessary? Have I missed something in the series that might shed light on this? Things I have noticed: MP Evas suicide by piercing their cores (so there may be a soul in there - or they may just be coincidentally piercing their core to get to the plug; the profile of the stuck MP Eva with the moon behind at 1:12 of EoE shows that possibility). MP Evas when dying don't display any pyrotechnics which might suggest there's a soul in them (although neither do some Angels or Eva 02 - but I don't recall seeing her core get breached specifically). If there are souls in the MP Evas, it seems it would be best that they are "in hiding" so that commands can be directed to them via "Adam's thought patterns" in the dummy plug without interference. edit: typo

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 26, 2009 1:46 pm Assuming Kaworu wasn't lying, the ATF directly requires a soul. Adam only has one soul, so it's not possible that the dummy plugs used in the MPEs actually contained sentient Kaworus... more likely blanks with imprinted memory, just enough to 'turn the key' as it were (which would also explain why they generally suck at fighting). Thus there must be souls in the MPEs: either willing donors from Seele's ranks or conscripted labor.

[]: Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Jan 26, 2009 1:53 pm NemZ wrote: conscripted labor.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Jan 26, 2009 1:57 pm The MP Eva's run on the Dummy Plug system or they all have Kaworu or "Adam's" soul within them...How that works is being discussed within another topic. Its like the soul is a piece of cake and everyone is cutting some off and passing it around.

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 26, 2009 2:26 pm Eva-01 has Yui's soul but has a Rei dummy plug in them. Most people posit that Adam's soul was not part of the dummy plug, as Ritsuko put it, you can't copy souls. The cores themselves contain a soul separate of that of the pilot. One hint is when the cores are broken near the end of EoE and it glows red as if the soul was leaving. But take what Ritsuko says about it being impossible to copy a soul and that the dummy plugs didn't have Rei's soul (Rei was the only one), and that Eva-01's activation with the dummy plug had a resident soul in the core (Yui), I think it's safe to assume that the Kaworu dummy plugs don't have souls, and the cores do. Nobody knows whose they are, like NemZ says, they might have been taken against their will (just need to find suitable and kill them). As an interesting note, there was some wild speculation many years ago involving the scene that was removed in EoE where Kensuke tells Shinji that they were all heading to Germany (Toji playing basketball in a wheel chair), which lead to the possibility that the students from 2-A WERE the souls in the MPE. But, wild speculation, that's all.

[]: Post by VoidEater » Mon Jan 26, 2009 2:50 pm Thanks all - yes, they have ATF, therefore they have soul...I hadn't heard about the deleted scene before, that is suggestive at the very least.

[]: Post by Xard » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:03 pm I think scene was deleted for the very reason what it seems to suggest.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:13 pm I think it should have been left it for that very reason. Oh, the tragic irony never stops. Hell, maybe Hikari is the one who tossed that lance!

[]: Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon Jan 26, 2009 6:31 pm It's fun to dream. *Imagines Toji and Kensuke eating Asuka's entrails*

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 26, 2009 6:36 pm Toji is the one she actually had a multiple-swing swordfight with, and Kensuke got the needles in his head. The ones she was especially mean to (knee through the head, cut in half, jam my arm though you to get to someone more interesting) were the unnamed members of Shinji's fan club.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 26, 2009 6:39 pm ^ I like the way you think. Where pray tell would Hikari fit into that scheme of things? In seriousness though: I don't think the MPs had the souls of the 2-A classmates. Instead, I think they were just souls of random Seele employees or something. That cut scene with wheelchair Toji was probably deleted for a reason afterall.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Jan 26, 2009 6:49 pm Actually, Toji dying is one of the things that I believe the manga did better than the show. And mentioned above, Hikari would be the one that just lost a leg and probably threw that headshot lance.

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 26, 2009 6:50 pm I think I had read that it wasn't included because it didn't fit with the movie. Firstly, in episode 24, Shinji says: Episode 24 wrote: Shinji: Everyone, including Toji and Kensuke, lost their homes and left. My friends... I don't have anyone I can call a friend anymore. There's no one.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Jan 26, 2009 7:00 pm When I first thought of the MP Evangelions having possible souls... The first thing that came to mind was...<no> Mentally Disabled Children with the Urge to Kill.

[]: Post by Xard » Tue Jan 27, 2009 12:29 pm Ornette wrote: I think I had read that it wasn't included because it didn't fit with the movie. Firstly, in episode 24, Shinji says: Episode 24 wrote: Shinji: Everyone, including Toji and Kensuke, lost their homes and left. My friends... I don't have anyone I can call a friend anymore. There's no one.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jan 27, 2009 2:34 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: When I first thought of the MP Evangelions having possible souls... The first thing that came to mind was...<no> Mentally Disabled Children with the Urge to Kill.

[]: Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 27, 2009 9:53 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: I still believe they are just Kaworu copies

[]: Post by Oedipusfoot » Mon Feb 02, 2009 6:52 pm Are there bodies in the Dummy Plug entry plugs?

[]: Post by thewayneiac » Mon Feb 02, 2009 7:16 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: Are there bodies in the Dummy Plug entry plugs?

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 02, 2009 8:13 pm Good point, although that's dependent on how the clones in the tank were numbered. If Rei 1 was numberless, and Rei 2 being the first proper clone was "Rei 00", then the next number would be "Rei 01" (aka Rei 3) and the final two, "Rei 02" and "Rei 03" would be the ones in the dummy plugs for Eva-01 and Eva-02. I admit that this is rather convoluted, so it's likely that your answer- the simpler one- may be right.
